text.tspan: More Free Trade/Integration
Status Quo
Scenario 2 - Deepening Integration
Scenario 1 - USMCA + Some Improvements (Base Case)
Scenario 3 - Status Quo (Negotiation Breakdown or Kick the Can)
Summary
-US & Mexico formalize alignment on China policy
-Modernizations & expansions on topics like AI, labor
Fentanyl-linked IEEPA tariffs walked back given
substantial tangible progress
-More robust institutional framework than Scenario 1
-Narrower review, addresses some of the outstanding issues (auto rules of
origin, updates on digital trade/AI) but without adding substantial chapters
-Modest side agreements that are improvements to existing structure, still
requiring Congressional approval
-Mexico either unable to meet US requests on China investment or unwilling to legislate
them in the deal
-Other side issues derail the broader review, leading all parties to maintain status quo
-The whole deal remains intact, but review is limited aside from just kicking the can down
the road, outstanding disputes not resolved
-IEEPA tariffs remain in place
Timing Expectation
June/July 2026 plus a few months for approval
June/July 2026, or later if negotiations continue alongside broader treaty
approval
End of 2026 or beyond, depending on potential indications of withdrawal
Effective Tariff Rate
Slight downside to existing effective tariff rate as tariffs
on non-USMCA compliant goods brought down from
current levels
Keeping USMCA exemption means overall effective tariff rate stays around
current rate (low single digit)
Upside risk to effective tariff rate as non-USMCA compliant goods are tariffed at a higher
rate
Congressional Approval Required?
Yes
Possible, depending on scope of additions
No
